channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Valve release the Steam Controller's CAD files, so you can channel your inner Archie from Balamory and design mods or accessories for it
item.content:encoded:
Take a Steam Controller and this thing I've made to stick to it, and hey, it's a Steam Controller with a thing sticking to it

The Steam Controller's now out in the wild, and it's so nice, we've written some impressions of it twice. If just having the thing in your hands (assuming you grabbed one in time) isn't enough for you, you'll probably be glad to know that Valve have shared the CAD files for it, meaning techies who fancy modding or designing accessories for it can get cracking.

"Today we're making the CAD files for the external shell (surface topology) of Steam Controller and Puck available for download under a Creative Commons license," Valve wrote in a quick announcement post. "This includes an STP model of each, an STL model of each, and an engineering drawing with critical features/keep outs for each."

Dipping into the read me file that comes with the files, which you can find here, Valve added that while "your Steam Controller is yours, and you have the right to do with it what you want", they "highly recommend you leave it to professionals". "Any damage you do will not be covered by your warranty – but more importantly, you might break your Steam Controller, or even get hurt," the Steamyfolks said. "Be careful, and have fun."

So, maybe don't go wild if you're not a seasoned inventor like Archie from classic British kids show Balamory. These files being shared should also result in plenty of unofficial Steam Controller accessories made by other people hitting the shelves at some point soon, and as our very own James has shown in the past, you might not regret buying such things (Disclaimer: You also very much might regret buying such things).

While the Steam Machine's still currently lurking in the priceless ether thanks to RAMnarök, Valve will also be releasing CAD models for that too once it arrives. In that case, custom front panel designs will be the obvious mod of choice out of the gate. After all, who could resisty printing a big picture of Gabe Newell's face, an anime girl, or an anime girl with Gabe Newell's face on the front of their new games box?



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/valve-release-the-steam-controllers-cad-files-so-you-can-channel-your-inner-archie-from-balamory-and-design-mods-or-accessories-for-it
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Thief, Dishonored and Philip Marlowe combine in Noirmancer, a stealth sim that lets you wipe minds with a snap of your fingers
item.content:encoded:
Oh heck, colleagues, we got one: a promising videogame. Noirmancer is a first-person single-player stealth sim, heavily inspired by the original Thief, in which you are a hard-boiled sleuth with supernatural abilities. Think Humphrey Bogart, if Humphrey Bogart were a Dishonored character. Here are some things you can do: swim in midair, transform into vapour, stun alerted guards with pew-pew gestures, and chuck down telekinetic mats to serve as (usefully sound-absorbing) platforms.

It might sound like a jumble, like one of those Marvel comicbook movesets they have to prune severely for the sake of a coherent film adaptation, but the whimsical stage magician hand animations and midnight jazz SFX absolutely sell the package. Instead of swinging a blackjack (noirjack?), you'll put mooks to sleep with a snap of your gloved fingers and a rattle of cymbals. Forget Bogart - this is Penn or Teller, whichever of them has springier knees.

That said, you'll often fall back on more elementary, non-magical stealth gambits, like opening windows slowly to minimise noise, eavesdropping for hints, and identifying shadowy paths through well-lit areas using your visibility indicator in bottom right. All this takes place in a city after sundown, a mildly synesthetic world of white outlines and rippling footsteps. The missions offer a variety of safes to crack and filing cabinets to rifle through; if you're extra nosy, you might find ways to exploit "the back-door dealings of important people".

There's a 2-4 hour alpha demo for Noirmancer on Itch.io, with a full Steam release to follow (no date yet). One goal of the alpha is to test out what developer DistractedMOSFET considers to be Noirmancer's boldest idea: "a stealth game where you MUST erase memories in order to de-alert the situation."

I've had a quick play of the demo, and I'm still making my mind up about the memory wipe stuff. I'm currently preoccupied with using telekinetic mats for parkouring purposes, which reminds me of wielding the foam gun in Arkane's Prey. Come to think of it, this game could use a Mimic power, if it doesn't already have one. I would like to be a fedora in this game. Perhaps one of those roaming guards could absent-mindedly put me on.

Prefer to infiltrate in twos or threes? Please rub this piece of innocent blank paper with your cursor. It harbours the release date for Thick as Thieves, the new stealth sim from Warren Spector and Otherside.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/thief-dishonored-and-philip-marlowe-combine-in-noirmancer-a-stealth-sim-that-lets-you-wipe-minds-with-a-snap-of-your-fingers
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
